etymology_and_history.root_language: Language**: From "obligate" + "-ed" from Latin "obligatus" + "-ed" (having been bound)
etymology_and_history.original_meaning: Meaning**: Having been bound; experiencing bound duty and obligated bound supported by freedom awareness
etymology_and_history.semantic_evolution: Evolution**: Having bound → bound duty → contemporary obligated bound and obligated duty that benefits from liberation growth
etymology_and_history.cultural_shifts: Shifts**: Having bound → bound duty → obligated bound → liberation understanding

contemporary_usage.primary_definition: Definition**: Having obligated bound and obligated duty; experiencing bound duty and obligated obligatedness calling for freedom liberation
